name: Dobutamine
executiveSummary: Synthetic catecholamine with inotropic activity. Selective action on β1 receptors.
indications:
## Main indications

Synthetic catecholamine with inotropic activity. Selective action on β1 receptors.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.presentation: Ampoule or vial for IV infusion per institutional presentation.
adulto.reconstitution: Reconstitute per prescribing information; dilute in 5% dextrose or 0.9% NaCl.
adulto.infusionRate: Titrate according to BP and perfusion
adulto.administration: Continuous IV infusion pump; central line preferred.
adulto.compatibility: Verify in-line compatibility with other vasopressors.
adulto.notes: Monitor HR, invasive BP, urine output, and peripheral perfusion.

pediatrico.reconstitution: Reconstitute per prescribing information; dilute in 5% dextrose or 0.9% NaCl.
pediatrico.dose: 2-20 µg/kg/min, Maximum dose in adults: 40 µg/kg/min
pediatrico.infusionRate: Titrate according to BP and perfusion
pediatrico.compatibility: Verify in-line compatibility with other vasopressors.
pediatrico.notes: For IV administration dilute in normal saline or 5% dextrose with a maximum concentration of 5 mg/mL. Dilution is stable 24 h. Incompatible with alkaline solutions. Compatible with: dopamine, epinephrine, vecuronium, isoproterenol, and lidocaine. Do not administer to patients with dynamic left ventricular outflow obstruction. Pink coloration indicates mild oxidation but no loss of potency

neonatal.presentation: Ampoule or vial for IV infusion per institutional presentation.
neonatal.reconstitution: Reconstitute per prescribing information; dilute in 5% dextrose or 0.9% NaCl.
neonatal.dose: 2–10 mcg/kg/min in NICU under specialized prescription.
neonatal.infusionRate: Titrate according to BP and perfusion
neonatal.administration: Continuous IV infusion pump; central line preferred.
neonatal.compatibility: Verify in-line compatibility with other vasopressors.
neonatal.notes: Monitor HR, invasive BP, urine output, and peripheral perfusion.

stability:
## Stability

- Use within 24 h; protect from light per guide.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Anxiety, confusion, weakness, nausea, vomiting, reflex bradycardia. Arrhythmias and tachycardia with high doses.
